rulings.Case Title: ELIDA BARILLAS et al VS. AVAIRPROS SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Pltf'S Secodn Request For Production Of Documents To Deft
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 10, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFFS ELIDA BARILLAS, MARIO GOMEZ Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff's Second Request For Production Of Documents To Defendant. PLAINTIFFS MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SECOND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT SHOULD PRODUCE DOCUMENTS OR EXPLICITLY STATE (WITH VERIFICATION) THAT THEY DO NOT EXIST OR NO LONGER EXIST. IF THEY NO LONGER EXIST, DEFENDANT MUST PROVIDE AN EXPLANATION AS TO WHY THEY NO LONGER EXIST. REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS DENIED. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: CONCORDE EQUITY 11, LLC VS. Q-BLACK, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Restrain Parties From Disposing Of Collateral And Assets
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 10, 2011, line 1, PLAINTIFF CONCORDE EQUITY II, LLC Motion For Preliminary Injunction To Restrain Parties From Disposing Of Collateral And Assets. Plaintiff¿s Motion for Preliminary Injunction is denied. Injunctive Relief is rarely granted where a suit for damages provides a clear remedy. A damage remedy in this case is not precluded by law. While both Plaintiff and Intervenors have substantial claims, there are serious doubts that Plaintiff can demonstrate he has a reasonable probability of prevailing. Moreover, in weighing the respective hardships of the parties, it appears that Intervenors will suffer greater harm if the Preliminary Injunction is granted. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW M. KING VS. FREMONT INVESTMENT & LOAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Signature Group Holdings, Inc., Successor In Interest To Fremont Reorganizing Corporation Fka Fremont Investment & Loan Motion To The Strike First Amended Complaint (Per Ex Parte Order)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 10, 2011, line 4, Defendant Signature Group Holdings, Inc., Successor In Interest To Fremont Reorganizing Corporation Fka Fremont Investment & Loan Motion To The Strike First Amended Complaint. Defendants' Motion to Strike is granted without leave to amend. No Opposition was filed and the First Amended Complaint does not provide a factual basis to support the claim for punitive damages. =(302/LMG)

rulings.Case Title: ANDREW M. KING VS. FREMONT INVESTMENT & LOAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Signature Group Holdings, Inc., Successor In Interest To Fremont Reorganizing Corporation Fka Fremont Investment & Loan Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint (Per Ex Parte Order)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 10, 2011, line 4, Defendant Signature Group Holdings, Inc., Successor In Interest To Fremont Reorganizing Corporation Fka Fremont Investment & Loan Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint. Defendant's Demurrer to the 1st cause of action (declaratory relief) is sustained without leave to amend. This claim is the subject of claims that already exist in the complaint and cannot be used to give plaintiff a second cause of action for determination of identical issues. The Demurrer to the 2nd cause of action (CCC § 2923.52 (CFPA)) is sustained without leave to amend. This statute has been repealed. The Demurrer to the 3rd cause of action (Fraud) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plea facts with particularity to support a claim of fraud. Plaintiff must also plead facts which state why this claim falls within the established time limitations. The Demurrer to the 4th cause of action (implied good faith and fair dealing) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff needs to remove the tort recovery allegations and allege breach of some fundamental state policy following execution of the K, not before. Plaintiff must also plead facts which state why this claim falls within the established time limitations. The Demurrer to the 5th cause of action (unfair business practice) is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must more specifically plead fact and the actual practices that were fraudulent, unlawful or unfair and who actually committed the practices. The Demurrer to the 6th cause of action (CCC § 1918.5 - 1921.1920) is sustained without leave to amend. This is barred by the Statute of Limitations. The Demurrer to the 7th cause of action (Breach of Fiduciary Duty) is sustained without leave to amend. The lender/borrower relationship is not fiduciary in nature. The Demurrer to the 8th cause of action (Quiet Title) is sustained with leave to amend. Tender must be pled where a mortgager seeks to set aside a voidable
